spec_match.rationale: The demo uses <permission> elements under the hood which represents a completely different feature (HTMLPermissionElement) instead of <usermedia>.

interactive.rationale: Allows starting the camera, switching tracks, capturing a photo, enumerating devices, and inspecting settings.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page explicitly references and implements Chrome 150's <permission> element rather than the <usermedia> capability element that is the subject of this feature.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers device enumeration, track checking, canvas capture errors, and stopping streams properly.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link points to ID 5086597972893696 (Permission Element) instead of 4926233538330624 (UserMedia Capability Element).

design_system.rationale: Uses the showcase styles and layout constructs cleanly.

openQuestions.title: Align concept with <usermedia> instead of <permission> element
openQuestions.detail: This concept is in the wrong folder or represents a different feature; it implements HTMLPermissionElement instead of the UserMedia capability element. It should be completely rewritten or moved to a permission-element feature folder, and replaced with a stream capture demo using <usermedia>.

summary: An interactive, well-designed demo that unfortunately focuses on the HTMLPermissionElement (<permission> element) rather than the HTMLUserMediaElement (<usermedia> element) which is the subject of this feature.
